title: Authors and Citation
text: Adrián Cidre González. Author, maintainer. Egor Kotov. Author. Rafael H. M. Pereira. Author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Cidre González , Kotov E, Pereira R (2026). duckh3: H3 extension 'DuckDB'. R package version 0.1.0, https://cidree.github.io/duckh3/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {duckh3: H3 extension of 'DuckDB'},   author = {Adrián {Cidre González} and Egor Kotov and Rafael H. M. Pereira},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://cidree.github.io/duckh3/}, }

title: H3 extension of DuckDB
text: 🦆 {duckh3} work progress! ’re actively building refining package — function names, arguments, behaviour may change API matures. Feedback bug reports welcome via GitHub Issues. Thanks early user! 🙏 {duckh3} provides fast, memory-efficient functions analysing manipulating large spatial non-spatial datasets using H3 hierarchical indexing system R. bridges DuckDB’s H3 extension R’s data spatial ecosystems — particular {duckspatial}, {dplyr}, {sf} — can leverage DuckDB’s analytical power without leaving familiar R workflow.

title: H3 extension of DuckDB
text: {duckh3} operates regular R data frames, tibbles, dbplyr lazy tables, duckspatial_df objects. Unlike purely spatial workflows, H3 operations require data spatial. table longitude/latitude columns, existing H3 index column, valid starting point. DuckDB connection used, H3 operations run inside connection H3 extension enabled, letting DuckDB apply query optimisations data reaches R. Results returned lazily materialised explicitly collect . addition, {duckh3} registers set DuckDB macros default connection load time, making H3 functions available directly inside dplyr::mutate() lazy tables — wrapper function needed. Note work lazy tables, regular data frames.

title: H3 extension of DuckDB
text: functions follow ddbh3_*() prefix (DuckDB H3), structured around : ddbh3_lonlat_to_*() — longitude/latitude coordinates H3 representations ddbh3_points_to_*() — spatial point geometries H3 representations ddbh3_get_*() — retrieve H3 cell properties (resolution, parent, children, vertices…) ddbh3_is_*() — check properties H3 indexes (valid, pentagon, Class III…) ddbh3_h3_to_*() — convert H3 cells representations

title: H3 extension of DuckDB
text: Install latest GitHub version: Install development version (may unstable):
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("Cidree/duckh3") pak::pak("Cidree/duckh3@dev")

title: H3 extension of DuckDB
text: central design principle {duckh3} H3 operation can used different ways depending data stored output format need.

title: H3 extension of DuckDB
text: Inputs can plain R data frames, lazy dbplyr tables, sf objects, duckspatial_df objects, making {duckh3} easy integrate non-spatial spatial pipelines.

title: H3 extension of DuckDB
text: Bug reports, feature requests, pull requests welcome! Raise issue Open pull request

what: Create a DuckDB connection with spatial and h3 extensions — ddbh3_create_conn
title: Create a DuckDB connection with spatial and h3 extensions — ddbh3_create_conn
text: creates DuckDB connection, installs loads spatial h3 extensions

title: Create a DuckDB connection with spatial and h3 extensions — ddbh3_create_conn
code: ddbh3_create_conn(dbdir = "memory", threads = NULL, memory_limit_gb = NULL)

title: Create a DuckDB connection with spatial and h3 extensions — ddbh3_create_conn
text: dbdir String. Either "tempdir", "memory", file path .duckdb .db extension. Defaults "memory". threads Integer. Number threads use. NULL (default), setting changed, DuckDB engine use available cores detects (warning, shared HPC nodes detected number cores might total number cores node, per-job allocation). memory_limit_gb Numeric. Memory limit GB. NULL (default), setting changed, DuckDB engine use 80% available operating system memory detects (warning, shared HPC nodes detected memory might full node memory, per-job allocation).

title: Create a DuckDB connection with spatial and h3 extensions — ddbh3_create_conn
text: duckdb_connection

title: Create a DuckDB connection with spatial and h3 extensions — ddbh3_create_conn
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # load packages library(duckspatial) library(duckh3)  # create a duckdb database in memory conn <- ddbh3_create_conn(dbdir = "memory")  # create a duckdb database in disk conn <- ddbh3_create_conn(dbdir = "my_database.duckdb")  # create an in-memory connection with 1 thread and 2GB memory limit conn <- ddbh3_create_conn(threads = 1, memory_limit_gb = 2) ddbs_stop_conn(conn) } # }

what: Get the position of an H3 cell within its parent — ddbh3_get_child_pos
title: Get the position of an H3 cell within its parent — ddbh3_get_child_pos
text: Get position H3 cell indexes stored strings unsigned 64-bit integers (UBIGINT) within parent cell specified resolution. position zero-based index among children parent cell.

title: Get the position of an H3 cell within its parent — ddbh3_get_child_pos
code: ddbh3_get_child_pos(   x,   h3 = "h3string",   resolution = 8,   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   new_column = "h3child_pos",   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Get the position of an H3 cell within its parent — ddbh3_get_child_pos
text: x Input spatial data. Can : duckspatial_df object (lazy spatial data frame via dbplyr) sf object tbl_lazy dbplyr character string naming table/view conn Data returned object. h3 name column x containing H3 strings H3 unsigned 64-bit integers (UBIGINT) resolution number specifying resolution level H3 string (0 15) conn connection object DuckDB database. NULL, function runs temporary DuckDB database. name character string length one specifying name table, character string length two specifying schema table names. NULL (default), function returns result sf object new_column Name new column create input data. NULL, function return vector result overwrite Boolean. whether overwrite existing table exists. Defaults FALSE. argument ignored name NULL. quiet logical value. TRUE, suppresses informational messages. Defaults FALSE.

title: Get the position of an H3 cell within its parent — ddbh3_get_child_pos
text: tbl_lazy x spatial, duckspatial_df x spatial (e.g. sf duckspatial_df). Alternatively, creates table connection name provided, returns TRUE invisibly.

title: Get the position of an H3 cell within its parent — ddbh3_get_child_pos
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ## Load needed packages library(duckh3) library(dplyr)  ## Load example data points_tbl <- read.csv(   system.file("extdata/example_pts.csv", package = "duckh3") )  ## Add H3 string column points_tbl <- ddbh3_lonlat_to_h3(points_tbl, resolution = 6)  ## Get position relative to resolution 4 ddbh3_get_child_pos(points_tbl, resolution = 4)  ## Add using mutate points_tbl |>    mutate(child_pos = ddbh3_get_child_pos(h3string, 4)) } # }

what: Get parent and children H3 cells — ddbh3_get_hierarchy
title: Get parent and children H3 cells — ddbh3_get_hierarchy
text: Get parent children H3 cells H3 cell indexes stored strings unsigned 64-bit integers (UBIGINT) specified resolution: ddbh3_get_parent(), ddbh3_get_center_child(), ddbh3_get_children(), ddbh3_get_n_children().

title: Get parent and children H3 cells — ddbh3_get_hierarchy
code: ddbh3_get_parent(   x,   h3 = "h3string",   resolution = 8,   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   new_column = "h3parent",   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )  ddbh3_get_children(   x,   h3 = "h3string",   resolution = 8,   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   new_column = "h3children",   nested = FALSE,   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )  ddbh3_get_n_children(   x,   h3 = "h3string",   resolution = 8,   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   new_column = "h3n_children",   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )  ddbh3_get_center_child(   x,   h3 = "h3string",   resolution = 8,   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   new_column = "h3center_child",   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Get parent and children H3 cells — ddbh3_get_hierarchy
text: x Input spatial data. Can : duckspatial_df object (lazy spatial data frame via dbplyr) sf object tbl_lazy dbplyr character string naming table/view conn Data returned object. h3 name column x containing H3 strings H3 unsigned 64-bit integers (UBIGINT) resolution number specifying resolution level H3 string (0 15) conn connection object DuckDB database. NULL, function runs temporary DuckDB database. name character string length one specifying name table, character string length two specifying schema table names. NULL (default), function returns result sf object new_column Name new column create input data. NULL, function return vector result overwrite Boolean. whether overwrite existing table exists. Defaults FALSE. argument ignored name NULL. quiet logical value. TRUE, suppresses informational messages. Defaults FALSE. nested Logical. TRUE, children returned nested list column (one row per parent cell). FALSE (default), result unnested child cell occupies row.

title: Get parent and children H3 cells — ddbh3_get_hierarchy
text: tbl_lazy x spatial, duckspatial_df x spatial (e.g. sf duckspatial_df). Alternatively, creates table connection name provided, returns TRUE invisibly.

title: Get parent and children H3 cells — ddbh3_get_hierarchy
text: four functions differ type related cell retrieve: ddbh3_get_parent() returns parent cell coarser resolution ddbh3_get_center_child() returns center child cell finer resolution ddbh3_get_children() returns children cells finer resolution ddbh3_get_n_children() returns number children cells finer resolution, without computing

title: Get parent and children H3 cells — ddbh3_get_hierarchy
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ## Load needed packages library(duckh3) library(dplyr)  ## Load example data points_tbl <- read.csv(   system.file("extdata/example_pts.csv", package = "duckh3") )  ## Add h3 strings points_tbl <- ddbh3_lonlat_to_h3(points_tbl, resolution = 8)  ## GET PARENTS ----------  ## Get resolution-7 parent points_parent_tbl <- ddbh3_get_parent(points_tbl, resolution = 7)  ## Check the resolution ddbh3_get_resolution(   points_parent_tbl,   h3 = "h3parent" )  ## Add with mutate points_tbl |>    mutate(parent4 = ddbh3_get_parent(h3string, 4))  ## GET CHILDREN ----------  ## Get level 9 children children_9_tbl <- ddbh3_get_children(points_tbl, resolution = 9)  ## Get level 9 children in a nested list children_9_nested_tbl <- ddbh3_get_children(points_tbl, resolution = 9, nested = TRUE)  ## Add with mutate (nested) points_tbl |>    mutate(children9 = ddbh3_get_children(h3string, 9))  ## Add with mutate (unnested) points_tbl |>    mutate(children9 = ddbh3_get_children(h3string, 9)) |>    mutate(children9 = unnest(children9))  ## GET CENTER CHILD ------  ## Get the center child of res 10 (1 child per row) center_child_10_tbl <- ddbh3_get_center_child(points_tbl, resolution = 10)  ## Add with mutate points_tbl |>    mutate(center = ddbh3_get_center_child(h3string, 9))  ## NUMBER OF CHILDREN -----  ## How many children of level 10 does each level 8 have? n_children_tbl <- ddbh3_get_n_children(points_tbl, resolution = 10)  ## Add with mutate points_tbl |>    mutate(n_children = ddbh3_get_n_children(h3string, 15))  } # }

what: Get the icosahedron faces of H3 cell indexes — ddbh3_get_icosahedron_faces
title: Get the icosahedron faces of H3 cell indexes — ddbh3_get_icosahedron_faces
text: Get icosahedron faces intersected H3 cell indexes stored strings unsigned 64-bit integers (UBIGINT). H3 cell maps onto one 20 faces underlying icosahedron used construct H3 grid.

title: Get the icosahedron faces of H3 cell indexes — ddbh3_get_icosahedron_faces
code: ddbh3_get_icosahedron_faces(   x,   h3 = "h3string",   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   new_column = "h3faces",   nested = FALSE,   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Get the icosahedron faces of H3 cell indexes — ddbh3_get_icosahedron_faces
text: x Input spatial data. Can : duckspatial_df object (lazy spatial data frame via dbplyr) sf object tbl_lazy dbplyr character string naming table/view conn Data returned object. h3 name column x containing H3 strings H3 unsigned 64-bit integers (UBIGINT) conn connection object DuckDB database. NULL, function runs temporary DuckDB database. name character string length one specifying name table, character string length two specifying schema table names. NULL (default), function returns result sf object new_column Name new column create input data. NULL, function return vector result nested Logical. TRUE, children returned nested list column (one row per parent cell). FALSE (default), result unnested child cell occupies row. overwrite Boolean. whether overwrite existing table exists. Defaults FALSE. argument ignored name NULL. quiet logical value. TRUE, suppresses informational messages. Defaults FALSE.

title: Get the icosahedron faces of H3 cell indexes — ddbh3_get_icosahedron_faces

title: Get the icosahedron faces of H3 cell indexes — ddbh3_get_icosahedron_faces
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ## Load needed packages library(duckh3) library(dplyr)  ## Load example data points_tbl <- read.csv(   system.file("extdata/example_pts.csv", package = "duckh3") )  ## Add H3 string column points_tbl <- ddbh3_lonlat_to_h3(points_tbl, resolution = 6)  ## Get faces (unnested) faces_tbl <- ddbh3_get_icosahedron_faces(points_tbl)  ## Get faces (nested) faces_nested_tbl <- ddbh3_get_icosahedron_faces(points_tbl, nested = TRUE)  ## Add using mutate (nested) points_tbl |>    mutate(faces = ddbh3_get_icosahedron_faces(h3string))  ## Add using mutate (unnested) points_tbl |>    mutate(faces = ddbh3_get_icosahedron_faces(h3string)) |>    mutate(faces_unnested = unnest(faces)) } # }

Defaults FALSE. argument ignored name NULL. quiet logical value. TRUE, suppresses informational messages. Defaults FALSE.

title: Convert H3 string or UBIGINT indexes to other representations — ddbh3_h3_to
text: tbl_lazy x spatial, duckspatial_df x spatial (e.g. sf duckspatial_df). Alternatively, creates table connection name provided, returns TRUE invisibly.

title: Convert H3 string or UBIGINT indexes to other representations — ddbh3_h3_to
text: four functions differ output format: ddbh3_h3_to_spatial() converts H3 indexes spatial hexagon polygons ddbh3_h3_to_lon() extracts longitude H3 cell centroid ddbh3_h3_to_lat() extracts latitude H3 cell centroid ddbh3_strings_to_bigint() converts H3 indexes unsigned 64-bit integers (UBIGINT) ddbh3_bigint_to_strings() converts H3 indexes strings (e.g. "8928308280fffff")

title: Convert H3 string or UBIGINT indexes to other representations — ddbh3_h3_to
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ## Load needed packages library(duckh3) library(duckspatial) library(dplyr)  ## Load example data points_tbl <- read.csv(   system.file("extdata/example_pts.csv", package = "duckh3") )  ## Add H3 string column points_tbl <- ddbh3_lonlat_to_h3(points_tbl, resolution = 6) |>    select(-lon, -lat)  ## TO LON/LAT ------------  ## Add longitude and latitude of the H3 string points_coords_tbl <- points_tbl |>    ddbh3_h3_to_lat() |>    ddbh3_h3_to_lon()  ## Add lon/lat with other names points_coords_2_tbl <- points_tbl |>    ddbh3_h3_to_lat(new_column = "latitude") |>    ddbh3_h3_to_lon(new_column = "longitude")  ## Add using mutate points_tbl |>    mutate(     lon = ddbh3_h3_to_lon(h3string),     lat = ddbh3_h3_to_lat(h3string)   )  ## TO SPATIAL -----------------  ## Convert h3 strings to spatial polygons points_ddbs <- ddbh3_h3_to_spatial(points_tbl)  ## Collect as sf points_sf <- ddbs_collect(points_ddbs)  ## FROM STRING TO UBIGINT -----  ## Add ubigint, and remove strings points_bigint_tbl <- ddbh3_strings_to_bigint(   points_tbl,    new_column = "h3_integers" ) |>    select(-h3string)  ## Add using mutate points_tbl |>    mutate(h3int = ddbh3_strings_to_bigint(h3string))  ## FROM UBIGINT TO STRING -----  ## Add column with strings points_strings_tbl <- ddbh3_bigint_to_strings(   points_bigint_tbl,    h3 = "h3_integers" )   ## Add using mutate points_bigint_tbl |>    mutate(h3string = ddbh3_bigint_to_strings(h3_integers))  } # }

what: Check properties of H3 cell indexes — ddbh3_is

title: Check properties of H3 cell indexes — ddbh3_is
code: ddbh3_is_pentagon(   x,   h3 = "h3string",   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   new_column = "ispentagon",   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )  ddbh3_is_h3(   x,   h3 = "h3string",   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   new_column = "ish3",   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )  ddbh3_is_res_class_iii(   x,   h3 = "h3string",   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   new_column = "isclassiii",   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )  ddbh3_is_vertex(   x,   h3vertex = "h3vertex",   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   new_column = "isvertex",   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Check properties of H3 cell indexes — ddbh3_is
text: x Input spatial data. Can : duckspatial_df object (lazy spatial data frame via dbplyr) sf object tbl_lazy dbplyr character string naming table/view conn Data returned object. h3 name column x containing H3 strings H3 unsigned 64-bit integers (UBIGINT) conn connection object DuckDB database. NULL, function runs temporary DuckDB database. name character string length one specifying name table, character string length two specifying schema table names. NULL (default), function returns result sf object new_column Name new column create input data. NULL, function return vector result overwrite Boolean. whether overwrite existing table exists. Defaults FALSE. argument ignored name NULL. quiet logical value. TRUE, suppresses informational messages. Defaults FALSE. h3vertex Name column containing H3 vertex strings. Defaults "h3vertex"

title: Check properties of H3 cell indexes — ddbh3_is
text: tbl_lazy x spatial, duckspatial_df x spatial (e.g. sf duckspatial_df). Alternatively, creates table connection name provided, returns TRUE invisibly.

title: Check properties of H3 cell indexes — ddbh3_is
text: functions check different properties H3 cell indexes, returning logical column: ddbh3_is_pentagon(): returns TRUE H3 cell one 12 pentagonal cells exist H3 resolution ddbh3_is_h3(): returns TRUE H3 cell index valid H3 cell ddbh3_is_res_class_iii(): returns TRUE H3 cell belongs Class III resolution (odd resolutions: 1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11, 13, 15) ddbh3_is_vertex(): returns TRUE index valid H3 vertex

title: Check properties of H3 cell indexes — ddbh3_is
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ## Load needed packages library(duckh3) library(dplyr)  ## Load example data points_tbl <- read.csv(   system.file("extdata/example_pts.csv", package = "duckh3") )  ## Add h3 strings points_tbl <- ddbh3_lonlat_to_h3(points_tbl, resolution = 8)  ## IS VALID H3 -------------  ## Check if h3 indexes are valid ddbh3_is_h3(points_tbl)  ## Check in mutate points_tbl |>   mutate(valid = ddbh3_is_h3(h3string))  ## IS PENTAGON -------------  ## Check if h3 indexes are pentagons ddbh3_is_pentagon(points_tbl)  ## Check in mutate points_tbl |>   mutate(is_pent = ddbh3_is_pentagon(h3string))  ## IS CLASS III ------------  ## Check if h3 indexes belong to a Class III resolution ddbh3_is_res_class_iii(points_tbl)  ## Check across multiple resolutions ddbh3_lonlat_to_h3(points_tbl, resolution = 7) |>   ddbh3_is_res_class_iii()  ## IS VERTEX ---------------  ## Get vertexes first vertex_tbl <- ddbh3_h3_to_vertex(points_tbl, n = 1)  ## Check if indexes are valid vertexes ddbh3_is_vertex(vertex_tbl, h3 = "h3vertex")  ## Check in mutate (mix of h3 cells and vertexes) vertex_tbl |>   mutate(     cell_valid  = ddbh3_is_h3(h3string),     vertex_valid = ddbh3_is_vertex(h3vertex)   ) } # }

what: Convert longitude and latitude to H3 cell representations — ddbh3_lonlat_to
title: Convert longitude and latitude to H3 cell representations — ddbh3_lonlat_to
text: Convert geographic coordinates (longitude latitude) H3 cell representations specified resolution, different output formats

code: ddbh3_lonlat_to_spatial(   x,   lon = "lon",   lat = "lat",   resolution = 8,   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )  ddbh3_lonlat_to_h3(   x,   lon = "lon",   lat = "lat",   resolution = 8,   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   new_column = "h3string",   h3_format = "string",   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Convert longitude and latitude to H3 cell representations — ddbh3_lonlat_to
text: x Input spatial data. Can : duckspatial_df object (lazy spatial data frame via dbplyr) sf object tbl_lazy dbplyr character string naming table/view conn Data returned object. lon name column x containing longitude lat name column x containing latitude resolution number specifying resolution level H3 string (0 15) conn connection object DuckDB database. NULL, function runs temporary DuckDB database. name character string length one specifying name table, character string length two specifying schema table names. NULL (default), function returns result sf object overwrite Boolean. whether overwrite existing table exists. Defaults FALSE. argument ignored name NULL. quiet logical value. TRUE, suppresses informational messages. Defaults FALSE. new_column Name new column create input data. NULL, function return vector result h3_format Character. format H3 cell index: string bigint

title: Convert longitude and latitude to H3 cell representations — ddbh3_lonlat_to
text: tbl_lazy x spatial, duckspatial_df x spatial (e.g. sf duckspatial_df). Alternatively, creates table connection name provided, returns TRUE invisibly.

title: Convert longitude and latitude to H3 cell representations — ddbh3_lonlat_to
text: three functions differ output format H3 cell index: ddbh3_lonlat_to_h3() returns H3 cell indexes strings (e.g. "8928308280fffff") unsigned 64-bit integers (UBIGINT) ddbh3_lonlat_to_spatial() returns H3 cells spatial hexagon polygons

title: Convert longitude and latitude to H3 cell representations — ddbh3_lonlat_to
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ## Load needed packages library(duckdb) library(duckh3) library(duckspatial) library(dplyr)  ## Load example data points_tbl <- read.csv(   system.file("extdata/example_pts.csv", package = "duckh3") )  ## Create a connection with spatial and h3 extensions conn <- ddbh3_create_conn()  ## TO H3 ------------  ## Add h3 strings as a new column (res 5) points_strings_5_tbl <- ddbh3_lonlat_to_h3(   points_tbl,   resolution = 5 )  ## Add h3 UBIGINT as a new column (res 8 by default) points_bigint_8_tbl <- ddbh3_lonlat_to_h3(   points_tbl,   new_column = "h3bigint",   h3_format  = "bigint" )  ## If column names are different from lon/lat: points_renamed <- rename(points_tbl, long = lon, lati = lat)  ddbh3_lonlat_to_h3(   points_renamed,   lon = "long",   lat = "lati",   resolution = 10 )   ## Create a new table in the connection ddbh3_lonlat_to_h3(   points_tbl,   conn = conn,   name = "points_strings_8" )  ## Open the created table lazily points_lazy <- dplyr::tbl(conn, "points_strings_8")  ## Read it in memory points_eager <- dbReadTable(conn, "points_strings_8")   ## TO SPATIAL -----------  ## Add h3 strings as a new column (res 5) points_5_ddbs <- ddbh3_lonlat_to_spatial(   points_tbl,   resolution = 5 )  ## Create a new table in the connection ddbh3_lonlat_to_spatial(   points_tbl,   conn = conn,   name = "points_strings_spatial" )  ## Open the created table lazily as_duckspatial_df("points_strings_spatial", conn)  ## Read it in memory as an sf object ddbs_read_table(conn, "points_strings_spatial")  } # }

what: Convert spatial points to H3 cell representations — ddbh3_points_to
title: Convert spatial points to H3 cell representations — ddbh3_points_to
text: Convert spatial point geometries H3 cell representations specified resolution. Input must duckspatial_df, sf, table point geometries

title: Convert spatial points to H3 cell representations — ddbh3_points_to
code: ddbh3_points_to_spatial(   x,   resolution,   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )  ddbh3_points_to_h3(   x,   resolution,   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   new_column = "h3string",   h3_format = "string",   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Convert spatial points to H3 cell representations — ddbh3_points_to
text: x Input spatial data. Can : duckspatial_df object (lazy spatial data frame via dbplyr) sf object tbl_lazy dbplyr character string naming table/view conn Data returned object. resolution number specifying resolution level H3 string (0 15) conn connection object DuckDB database. NULL, function runs temporary DuckDB database. name character string length one specifying name table, character string length two specifying schema table names. NULL (default), function returns result sf object overwrite Boolean. whether overwrite existing table exists. Defaults FALSE. argument ignored name NULL. quiet logical value. TRUE, suppresses informational messages. Defaults FALSE. new_column Name new column create input data. NULL, function return vector result h3_format Character. Output format H3 cell index. Either "string" (default) "bigint". used ddbh3_points_to_h3().

title: Convert spatial points to H3 cell representations — ddbh3_points_to
text: tbl_lazy x spatial, duckspatial_df x spatial (e.g. sf duckspatial_df). Alternatively, creates table connection name provided, returns TRUE invisibly.

title: Convert spatial points to H3 cell representations — ddbh3_points_to
text: two functions differ output format: ddbh3_points_to_h3() returns H3 cell index containing point, either string UBIGINT depending h3_format ddbh3_points_to_spatial() returns H3 cell hexagon polygon containing point spatial geometry

title: Convert spatial points to H3 cell representations — ddbh3_points_to
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ## Load needed packages library(duckh3) library(duckspatial)  ## Load example data points_tbl <- read.csv(   system.file("extdata/example_pts.csv", package = "duckh3") )  ## Convert to duckspatial_df points_ddbs <- ddbs_as_spatial(points_tbl)  ## TO H3 strings/ubigint ------------  ## Add column with h3 strings at resolution 8 points_strings_ddbs <- ddbh3_points_to_h3(points_ddbs, 8)  ## Add column with h3 ubigint at resolution 10 points_bigint_ddbs <- ddbh3_points_to_h3(   points_ddbs,    resolution = 8,   new_column = "h3bigint",   h3_format = "bigint" )  ## TO SPATIAL -----------------  ## Convert from POINTS to H3 POLYGONS of res 8 polygons_8_ddbs <- ddbh3_points_to_spatial(points_ddbs, 8)  ## Collect as sf polygons_8_sf <- ddbs_collect(polygons_8_ddbs) } # }

what: Convert H3 cell indexes to vertex representations — ddbh3_vertex
title: Convert H3 cell indexes to vertex representations — ddbh3_vertex
text: Convert H3 cell indexes stored strings unsigned 64-bit integers (UBIGINT) vertex representations

title: Convert H3 cell indexes to vertex representations — ddbh3_vertex
code: ddbh3_h3_to_vertex(   x,   h3 = "h3string",   n = 0,   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   new_column = "h3vertex",   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )  ddbh3_vertex_to_lon(   x,   h3vertex = "h3vertex",   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   new_column = "lon_vertex",   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )  ddbh3_vertex_to_lat(   x,   h3vertex = "h3vertex",   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   new_column = "lat_vertex",   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )  ddbh3_h3_to_vertexes(   x,   h3 = "h3string",   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   new_column = "h3vertex",   nested = FALSE,   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )  ddbh3_vertex_to_spatial(   x,   h3vertex = "h3vertex",   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Convert H3 cell indexes to vertex representations — ddbh3_vertex
text: x Input spatial data. Can : duckspatial_df object (lazy spatial data frame via dbplyr) sf object tbl_lazy dbplyr character string naming table/view conn Data returned object. h3 name column x containing H3 strings H3 unsigned 64-bit integers (UBIGINT) n Integer. Vertex number retrieve. Must range 0–5 hexagons 0–4 pentagons. used ddbh3_h3_to_vertex(). conn connection object DuckDB database. NULL, function runs temporary DuckDB database. name character string length one specifying name table, character string length two specifying schema table names. NULL (default), function returns result sf object new_column Name new column create input data. NULL, function return vector result overwrite Boolean. whether overwrite existing table exists. Defaults FALSE. argument ignored name NULL. quiet logical value. TRUE, suppresses informational messages. Defaults FALSE. h3vertex Name column containing H3 vertex strings. Defaults "h3vertex" nested Logical. TRUE, children returned nested list column (one row per parent cell). FALSE (default), result unnested child cell occupies row.

title: Convert H3 cell indexes to vertex representations — ddbh3_vertex
text: tbl_lazy x spatial, duckspatial_df x spatial (e.g. sf duckspatial_df). Alternatively, creates table connection name provided, returns TRUE invisibly.

title: Convert H3 cell indexes to vertex representations — ddbh3_vertex
text: functions cover full vertex workflow: ddbh3_h3_to_vertex() returns single vertex H3 cell, identified vertex number n (0–5 hexagons, 0–4 pentagons), H3 vertex string ddbh3_h3_to_vertexes() returns vertices H3 cell H3 vertex strings — either nested (one row per cell) unnested (one row per vertex) depending nested ddbh3_vertex_to_lat() returns latitude H3 vertex string ddbh3_vertex_to_lon() returns longitude H3 vertex string ddbh3_vertex_to_spatial() converts H3 vertex strings spatial point geometries. input column nested, vertices automatically unnested aggregated MULTIPOINT geometry per cell

title: Convert H3 cell indexes to vertex representations — ddbh3_vertex
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ## Load needed packages library(duckh3) library(duckspatial) library(dplyr)  ## Load example data points_tbl <- read.csv(   system.file("extdata/example_pts.csv", package = "duckh3") )  ## Add h3 strings points_tbl <- ddbh3_lonlat_to_h3(points_tbl, resolution = 8)  ## TO VERTEX ---------------  ## Add second vertex vertex_2_tbl <- ddbh3_h3_to_vertex(points_tbl, n = 2)  ## Add add vertexes (unnested) vertexes_tbl <- ddbh3_h3_to_vertexes(points_tbl)  ## Add add vertexes (nested) vertexes_nested_tbl <- ddbh3_h3_to_vertexes(points_tbl, nested = TRUE)  ## Add some vertexes with with mutate points_tbl |>    mutate(     v1 = ddbh3_h3_to_vertex(h3string, 1),     v3 = ddbh3_h3_to_vertex(h3string, 3)   )  ## VERTEX TO LON/LAT ------  ## Add coords coords_vertex_tbl <- vertex_2_tbl |>    ddbh3_vertex_to_lon(new_column = "lon_v2") |>    ddbh3_vertex_to_lat(new_column = "lat_v2")  ## Add coords in mutate vertex_2_tbl |>    mutate(     lon_v2 = ddbh3_vertex_to_lon(h3vertex),     lat_v2 = ddbh3_vertex_to_lat(h3vertex)   )  ## VERTEX TO SPATIAL ------  ## Convert unnested vertexes (returns POINTS) ddbh3_h3_to_vertexes(points_tbl) |>    ddbh3_vertex_to_spatial()   ## Convert nested vertexes (returns MULTIPOINTS) ddbh3_h3_to_vertexes(points_tbl, nested = TRUE) |>    ddbh3_vertex_to_spatial() } # }

title: duckh3 0.1.0
text: development.
